message:
fix(test): fix bugs in JSON logging implementation

Bugs fixed:
1. skip_test now uses TF_TEST_WAS_SKIPPED flag to prevent double
   test_result events (skip + pass) in JSON output
2. Custom test context (set_test_context) now actually included
   in JSON log output via TF_JSON_TEST_CONTEXT
3. Branch names in _json_git_state now properly escaped
4. Removed unused test_name parameter from log_test_start_json
   (test name comes from TF_CURRENT_TEST via _json_log)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-sv6v","title":"Implement retry with exponential backoff","description":"Retry with exponential backoff for transient failures.

Function: retry_with_backoff(cmd, max_attempts, base_delay)

Algorithm:
- Start with base_delay (default 1s)
- On failure: delay = base * 2^(attempt-1)
- Add jitter: +/- 25% randomization
- Continue until success or max_attempts

Use cases:
- API calls that timeout
- Rate limit recovery
- Network glitches

Implementation logs warnings on each retry, returns final exit code.

Acceptance: Works for common failure patterns, respects max attempts, jitter prevents thundering herd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:23.01899538-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:27:24.053201995-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:27:24.053201995-05:00","close_reason":"Added retry_with_backoff (exp backoff + jitter) and used for gh api GraphQL/rate_limit; fixed governor grep portability","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv6v","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:15.633158875-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:17.313860817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.883229859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vcr9","title":"Implement gh_actions execution from review plan","description":"Task: Implement gh_actions Execution

Purpose
-------
Execute GitHub mutations (comments, closes, labels) from the gh_actions
array in review-plan.json during the Apply phase.

gh_actions Schema
-----------------
{
  "gh_actions": [
    {"op": "comment", "target": "issue#42", "body": "Fixed in..."},
    {"op": "close", "target": "issue#42", "reason": "completed"},
    {"op": "label", "target": "issue#42", "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]},
    {"op": "comment", "target": "pr#15", "body": "Thank you..."}
  ]
}

Supported Operations
--------------------

1. comment
   Add comment to issue or PR
   gh issue comment N --body "..."
   gh pr comment N --body "..."

2. close
   Close issue or PR
   gh issue close N --reason "..."
   gh pr close N --comment "..."

3. label
   Add labels to issue
   gh issue edit N --add-label "label1,label2"

4. (merge - NOT SUPPORTED per policy)

Implementation
--------------

execute_gh_actions()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  local actions
  actions=$(jq -c .gh_actions[] "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null) || return 0
  
  while IFS= read -r action; do
    local op target
    op=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .op)
    target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
    
    case "$op" in
      comment) execute_comment "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      close)   execute_close "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      label)   execute_label "$repo_id" "$action" ;;
      *)
        log_warn "Unknown gh_action op: $op"
        ;;
    esac
  done <<< "$actions"

execute_comment()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target body type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  body=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .body)
  
  # Parse target: "issue#42" or "pr#15"
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Commenting on $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr comment "$number" -R "$repo_id" --body "$body"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_close()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target reason type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  reason=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".reason // \"completed\"")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Closing $target"
  
  case "$type" in
    issue)
      gh issue close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --reason "$reason"
      ;;
    pr)
      gh pr close "$number" -R "$repo_id" --comment "Closing: $reason"
      ;;
  esac
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

execute_label()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local target labels_json type number
  target=$(echo "$action" | jq -r .target)
  labels_json=$(echo "$action" | jq -r ".labels | join(\",\")")
  type="${target%%#*}"
  number="${target##*#}"
  
  log_step "Adding labels to $target: $labels_json"
  
  gh issue edit "$number" -R "$repo_id" --add-label "$labels_json"
  
  record_action_executed "$repo_id" "$action"

Action Recording
----------------
Track what was executed for audit:

record_action_executed()
  local repo_id="$1"
  local action="$2"
  
  local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/gh_actions.log"
  local ts=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
  
  echo "${ts}|${repo_id}|${action}" >> "$log_file"

Error Handling
--------------
- Log errors but continue with other actions
- Record failed actions in state
- Retry logic for transient failures
- Skip already-executed actions on resume

Testing
-------
- Mock gh commands for unit tests
- Verify comment body escaping
- Verify close reasons work
- Verify labels applied
- Test error handling

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Comments posted correctly
- [ ] Issues/PRs closed with reason
- [ ] Labels applied
- [ ] Actions logged for audit
- [ ] Errors handled gracefully
- [ ] Idempotent on retry","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:09.158053493-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:32:04.738399665-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented gh_actions execution with 9 functions: execute_gh_actions, execute_gh_action_{comment,close,label}, gh_action_already_executed, record_gh_action_log, parse_gh_action_target, canonicalize_gh_action, get_gh_actions_log_file. Features idempotence, audit logging, policy enforcement.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vcr9","depends_on_id":"bd-ldyv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.518493177-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vp2","title":"E2E: ru doctor workflow (diagnostics, exit codes)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:10:46.752673805-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T21:16:59.31292603-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T21:16:59.31292603-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive E2E test suite for ru doctor workflow with 14 test cases covering: basic functionality, git/gh/config/repos/projects checks, exit codes (0/3), output format, and status indicators"}
 {"id":"bd-wrfp","title":"Sub-epic: Test Framework Enhancement (Logging, Real Tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:52:37.044307629-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T22:55:59.439502448-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T22:55:59.439502448-05:00","close_reason":"Sub-epic scope defined: 4 tasks for JSON logging, test isolation, coverage tracking, parallel execution. Implementation begins now.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wrfp","depends_on_id":"bd-e1eo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:52:37.226007467-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -51,6 +51,7 @@ TF_TESTS_SKIPPED=0
 TF_ASSERTIONS_PASSED=0
 TF_ASSERTIONS_FAILED=0
 TF_CURRENT_TEST=""
+TF_TEST_WAS_SKIPPED="false"  # Flag for skip_test to communicate with run_test
 
 # Temp directory management
 TF_TEMP_DIRS=()
@@ -448,6 +449,7 @@ _json_git_state() {
     if command -v git &>/dev/null && git rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
         local branch commit dirty
         branch=$(git symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "detached")
+        branch=$(_json_escape "$branch")
         commit=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
         dirty="false"
         if ! git diff --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
@@ -517,6 +519,10 @@ _json_log() {
     if [[ -n "$TF_JSON_SUITE_NAME" ]]; then
         json+=",\"suite_name\":\"$TF_JSON_SUITE_NAME\""
     fi
+    # Add custom test context if set (not empty object)
+    if [[ -n "$TF_JSON_TEST_CONTEXT" && "$TF_JSON_TEST_CONTEXT" != "{}" ]]; then
+        json+=",\"context\":$TF_JSON_TEST_CONTEXT"
+    fi
 
     # Add key-value pairs
     while [[ $# -ge 2 ]]; do
@@ -580,9 +586,9 @@ log_suite_end_json() {
 }
 
 # Log test start (JSON)
-# Usage: log_test_start_json "test_name"
+# Usage: log_test_start_json
+# Note: test_name is automatically included via TF_CURRENT_TEST in _json_log
 log_test_start_json() {
-    local test_name="$1"
     _json_log "test_start" \
         "phase" "execute"
 }
@@ -1087,6 +1093,7 @@ run_test() {
     local test_start_us elapsed_us elapsed_ms
 
     TF_CURRENT_TEST="$test_name"
+    TF_TEST_WAS_SKIPPED="false"  # Reset skip flag
     test_start_us=$(_json_timestamp_us)
 
     if ! is_tap_mode; then
@@ -1095,14 +1102,18 @@ run_test() {
         echo "----------------------------------------"
     fi
 
-    # JSON: log test start
-    log_test_start_json "$test_name"
+    # JSON: log test start (test_name comes from TF_CURRENT_TEST)
+    log_test_start_json
 
     # Run the test
     if "$test_name"; then
-        elapsed_us=$(_json_elapsed_us "$test_start_us")
-        elapsed_ms=$((elapsed_us / 1000))
-        if [[ $TF_ASSERTIONS_FAILED -eq $failed_before ]]; then
+        # Check if test was skipped (skip_test was called)
+        if [[ "$TF_TEST_WAS_SKIPPED" == "true" ]]; then
+            # Skip already handled by skip_test - don't double-count
+            :
+        elif [[ $TF_ASSERTIONS_FAILED -eq $failed_before ]]; then
+            elapsed_us=$(_json_elapsed_us "$test_start_us")
+            elapsed_ms=$((elapsed_us / 1000))
             ((TF_TESTS_PASSED++))
             if is_tap_mode; then
                 _tap_ok "$test_name"
@@ -1112,6 +1123,8 @@ run_test() {
             # JSON: log test pass
             log_test_result_json "pass" "$elapsed_ms"
         else
+            elapsed_us=$(_json_elapsed_us "$test_start_us")
+            elapsed_ms=$((elapsed_us / 1000))
             ((TF_TESTS_FAILED++))
             if is_tap_mode; then
                 _tap_not_ok "$test_name" "assertions failed"
@@ -1135,6 +1148,7 @@ run_test() {
     fi
 
     TF_CURRENT_TEST=""
+    TF_TEST_WAS_SKIPPED="false"
     clear_test_context
 }
 
@@ -1143,11 +1157,14 @@ run_test() {
 skip_test() {
     local reason="${1:-}"
     ((TF_TESTS_SKIPPED++))
+    TF_TEST_WAS_SKIPPED="true"  # Signal to run_test that test was skipped
     if is_tap_mode; then
         _tap_skip "$TF_CURRENT_TEST" "$reason"
     else
         echo "${TF_YELLOW}SKIP${TF_RESET}: $TF_CURRENT_TEST${reason:+ ($reason)}"
     fi
+    # JSON: log test skip
+    log_test_result_json "skip" "0" "$reason"
     return 0
 }
